compiled_state.title: Tickets branch worktree out of sync with remote on session start
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Problem

When starting a new session, the local `tickets` branch worktree (`.tickets-tracker/`) can be out of sync with `origin/tickets`. The local branch only has the initial commit (`.gitignore`) while the remote has all actual ticket data. This causes all ticket commands (`ticket list`, `sprint-list-epics.sh`) to return empty results, silently hiding all existing tickets.

## Root Cause

The `.tickets-tracker/` worktree is checked out to the local `tickets` ref, which may not have been updated from `origin/tickets`. No automatic fetch/reset occurs on session start to ensure the local tickets branch matches the remote.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start a new session in a repo where the local `tickets` branch is behind `origin/tickets`
2. Run `.claude/scripts/dso ticket list` — returns `[]`
3. Run `git log tickets --oneline` — shows only the initial commit
4. Run `git log origin/tickets --oneline` — shows many ticket events

## Fix Applied (manual)

```bash
git fetch origin tickets
cd .tickets-tracker && git reset --hard origin/tickets
```

## Expected Behavior

Session startup (or the ticket CLI itself) should ensure the local tickets worktree is synced with the remote before returning results. A fetch + fast-forward (or reset) of the tickets branch should happen automatically.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). severity=1 (functional degradation - hides all tickets), complexity=1 (moderate - git worktree sync), environment=0 (local), intermittent=0 (deterministic). Route: BASIC investigation.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: intent-aligned (high confidence). Commit b9f91c35 and a5c450d7 confirm this is a defect — missing fetch in ticket-init.sh when local tickets branch already exists. No documentation justifies behavior as intentional.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: BASIC investigation complete. ROOT_CAUSE: The ticket CLI sync guard (ticket script lines 90-93) uses git log origin/tickets..tickets to detect local-ahead commits, but this check cannot distinguish between legitimate local-only commits and an unrelated orphan branch from a diverged initial setup. When the local tickets branch is an orphan (different root commit than origin/tickets), the check always finds commits, permanently blocking reset --hard and preserving the stale orphan. Fix: two-phase merge-base check in the sync guard. confidence: high. hypothesis: confirmed via merge-base --is-ancestor analysis.
comments.author: Main
